blog_posts.title: Designing Frictionless Onboarding for a Telehealth App
blog_posts.excerpt: How we reduced patient drop-off by 37% in the first session of a telehealth platform.
blog_posts.content:
In this UX case study, we walk through our process for redesigning the onboarding flow of a telehealth app used by thousands of patients.

We began with remote usability testing to understand where patients were abandoning the flow. The key issues were unclear progress feedback and overwhelming consent screens.

Our solution introduced chunked consent, progressive disclosure for medical forms, and a simplified identity verification step. After launch, completion rates improved by 37% and average time-to-complete dropped by 42 seconds.

blog_posts.title: Nudging Better Saving Habits in a Fintech App
blog_posts.excerpt: Behavioral design techniques that increased weekly savings actions by 24%.
blog_posts.content:
This UX case study explores how we used behavioral science to encourage consistent saving in a consumer fintech app.

We introduced goal-based saving jars, progress visualizations, and just-in-time prompts based on payday predictions. The design emphasized clarity and reduced cognitive load by limiting choices at each step.

Post-launch analytics showed a 24% increase in weekly savings actions and a 15% reduction in users withdrawing from their savings jars prematurely.

blog_posts.title: Personalized Learning Paths for an EdTech Platform
blog_posts.excerpt: Reimagining course discovery and progress for 120K+ active students.
blog_posts.content:
For a fast-growing EdTech platform, we redesigned the course discovery and learning path experience.

Through diary studies and analytics, we learned that learners struggled to understand which course to take next and how close they were to their goals. We introduced guided paths, clear skill outcomes, and a new progress tracker spanning multiple courses.

The redesign led to a 19% increase in course completion rates and higher satisfaction scores in quarterly NPS surveys.

blog_posts.title: Microinteractions That Make Interfaces Feel Effortless
blog_posts.excerpt: A practical guide to designing subtle moments that guide and reassure users.
blog_posts.content:
Microinteractions are small design moments with an outsized impact on perceived usability.

In this article we break down four categories of microinteractions—input feedback, status and progress, navigational hints, and error recovery—showing real examples from recent client projects.

We also cover motion guidelines and accessibility considerations to ensure these moments feel helpful, not distracting.

blog_posts.title: Leveling Up Accessibility in Product Teams
blog_posts.excerpt: Practical steps to embed accessibility into your design and development workflow.
blog_posts.content:
Accessibility is not a one-time checklist—it is an ongoing practice.

We share how we helped a SaaS team integrate accessibility into discovery, design, and QA. Topics include setting up shared color tokens, creating reusable accessible components, and running regular screen reader audits.

By the end of the project, critical accessibility issues dropped by 82% and the team felt confident shipping inclusive features.

blog_posts.title: From Pop-Up to Cult Favorite: Branding Ember Street Bistro
blog_posts.excerpt: How a cohesive brand system helped a street-food pop-up become a neighborhood staple.
blog_posts.content:
Ember Street Bistro began as a weekend pop-up and needed a brand that could scale into a permanent location.

We developed a flexible visual identity rooted in charcoal textures, bold typography, and a modular logo that works across signage, menus, and social content.

Within six months of launch, the bistro saw a 30% increase in direct reservations and higher recognition in local press.

blog_posts.title: Choosing the Right Design System for Your Team
blog_posts.excerpt: A framework for deciding when to adopt, adapt, or build a design system.
blog_posts.content:
Product teams often wrestle with whether to adopt an existing design system, customize a starter kit, or build their own.

We outline a simple three-path framework based on team maturity, product complexity, and roadmap constraints, with examples from SaaS, fintech, and non-profit clients.

The goal is not to pick the "best" system, but the option that will actually be maintained.

blog_posts.title: How We Curate Our Digital Design Resources
blog_posts.excerpt: Behind the scenes of our icon sets, UI kits, and mockups collection.
blog_posts.content:
Our shop focuses on production-ready resources that fit into real workflows, not just pretty visuals.

We share the criteria we use to curate and create icon sets, UI kits, mockups, and illustration packs, including accessibility, file organization, and license clarity.

You will also find tips for combining multiple resources into a cohesive visual language.

blog_posts.title: Running Effective Remote UX Tests on a Budget
blog_posts.excerpt: Tools, scripts, and templates we use to run studies without a lab.
blog_posts.content:
Remote research is now the default for many teams, but doing it well still requires rigor.

This post breaks down our approach to recruiting, scripting, and synthesizing remote usability tests for early-stage products.

We include templates you can adapt for your own sessions.

blog_posts.title: Designing a Medication Adherence App for Chronic Patients
blog_posts.excerpt: A cross-platform mobile app that supports patients with complex treatment plans.
blog_posts.content:
This mobile app design case study focuses on simplifying medication schedules for patients with chronic conditions.

We designed a schedule-first interface, structured reminders around routines, and added a caregiver sharing mode.

Clinical partners reported higher adherence and fewer support calls after rollout.

case_studies.title: Ember Street Bistro: From Pop-Up to Cult Favorite
case_studies.summary: A bold, fire-inspired identity for a modern street-food bistro.
case_studies.content:
Ember Street Bistro needed a flexible brand system that could stretch from food truck signage to a full-service restaurant.

We crafted a logo built from modular letterforms, a charcoal-and-ember color palette, and a photography direction that highlights the drama of open-flame cooking.

Deliverables included logo suite, menu system, storefront signage, social templates, and a 48-page brand guidelines document.

case_studies.title: Harbor Bowl: Coastal Ramen Bar Branding
case_studies.summary: A relaxed, coastal-inspired brand for a neighborhood ramen bar.
case_studies.content:
Harbor Bowl combined Japanese ramen with a coastal bar atmosphere. The visual identity needed to balance craft and approachability.

We created a hand-drawn logomark, a soft blue-and-sand color palette, and custom iconography for menu sections. A compact brand guide enabled the internal team to roll out social campaigns quickly.

case_studies.title: Verde Plant Kitchen: A Fresh Plant-Based Identity
case_studies.summary: Clean, flexible branding for a fast-casual plant-based restaurant.
case_studies.content:
Verde Plant Kitchen wanted to move beyond typical green-washing visuals.

We designed a typographic logo, a playful illustration system, and a color palette inspired by fresh produce. The brand guidelines documented logo usage, color, typography, and photography guidance for in-house marketing.

case_studies.title: PulseCare: Patient-Centered Telehealth App
case_studies.summary: A mobile app for managing appointments, medications, and remote visits.
case_studies.content:
PulseCare partnered with us to redesign their patient-facing mobile apps for iOS and Android.

We focused on consolidating scattered features into a clear home dashboard and prioritized accessibility for older adults.

Key outcomes included a 22% increase in repeat telehealth visits and higher CSAT scores across devices.

case_studies.title: SproutBank: Everyday Savings Fintech App
case_studies.summary: A mobile-first experience that helps users build sustainable saving habits.
case_studies.content:
SproutBank asked us to design a savings-focused mobile app that felt approachable to first-time investors.

We created a card-based interface for savings goals, real-time progress visualizations, and a gentle coaching tone with contextual tips.

Following launch, weekly savings actions increased while support tickets about confusion in the app dropped by 18%.

case_studies.title: LearnLoop: Student Hub for Hybrid Classrooms
case_studies.summary: A mobile app that unifies assignments, messaging, and resources for students.
case_studies.content:
LearnLoop needed a mobile app that could support students navigating hybrid and remote learning.

We designed a mobile-first experience with a unified timeline for assignments, class updates, and direct messages. Offline-first patterns ensured students with limited connectivity could still access critical materials.

Schools reported higher student engagement and fewer missed assignments after rollout.

case_studies.title: Aurora Commerce: High-Converting Shopify Theme
case_studies.summary: A flexible ecommerce template optimized for fashion and lifestyle brands.
case_studies.content:
Aurora Commerce is a conversion-focused ecommerce template with modular sections for lookbooks, cross-sells, and content-led landing pages.

We validated the design with usability testing sessions centered on product discovery and checkout flows.

case_studies.title: FolioFrames: Modular Portfolio Website
case_studies.summary: A portfolio template that adapts to agencies, studios, and freelancers.
case_studies.content:
FolioFrames was designed as a modular portfolio system with case-study storytelling at its core.

The template supports rich media, long-form storytelling, and structured project metadata that helps visitors filter quickly.

case_studies.title: MedSync: Connected Clinic Web Platform
case_studies.summary: A responsive web app for clinic staff to manage appointments and records.
case_studies.content:
MedSync is a staff-facing web platform for managing patient flow, scheduling, and records.

Our work included UX flows, responsive UI design, and design system foundations for the internal team.

case_studies.title: EduPath: Brand Strategy for a Learning Startup
case_studies.summary: Positioning and identity for an online learning recommendations startup.
case_studies.content: We helped EduPath clarify its positioning, define its brand voice, and create a scalable visual identity aligned with its product roadmap.

case_studies.title: WalletWave: Multi-Account Finance Dashboard
case_studies.summary: A responsive dashboard that consolidates accounts, budgets, and insights.
case_studies.content:
WalletWave needed a data-dense yet approachable dashboard experience for web and tablet.

We prioritized information hierarchy, customizable cards, and clear visualizations that support quick scanning.

case_studies.title: CampusWay: Orientation App for New Students
case_studies.summary: A lightweight mobile app that guides new students through their first weeks on campus.
case_studies.content:
CampusWay centralizes maps, events, and checklists for new university students.

Our design focused on wayfinding, clear timelines, and social features that help students feel connected early.
